$schema_description: Conference DRI Hub — Data Schema and Sample Data. This file defines the complete data model and includes sample data seeded from real CVPR 2026 and ICCV 2025 conference trackers at Applied Intuition.

Conference.name: string — display name (e.g. 'CVPR 2026')
Conference.niche: string — the domain/community this conference serves
Conference.privateEventName: string — name of the private event if applicable
Conference.actionItems: Record<string, ActionItem[]> — keys are phase names, values are arrays of tasks

Strategy.goals: string — what the company aims to achieve at this conference
Strategy.targetProfiles: string — who we want to meet/source
Strategy.keyMessages: string — talking points and positioning for booth and conversations
Strategy.successMetrics: string — how we measure whether this conference was worth it

Attendee.name: string
Attendee.team: string — e.g. 'Onroad/Perception', 'AI Research', 'AI Research/E2E', 'Offroad', 'Recruiting Program Management'
Attendee.ieeeMemberNumber: string — IEEE membership number (relevant for IEEE conferences)
Attendee.conferenceRegistered: boolean — whether early bird / standard registration is complete

Booth.materials: BoothMaterial[] — items to pack/ship with checkbox tracking
Booth.shifts: BoothShiftDay[] — the booth staffing schedule organized by day

BoothShiftSlot.isSpecialEvent: boolean — if true, this row is highlighted (e.g. BREAKFAST)
BoothShiftSlot.specialEventLabel: string — e.g. 'BREAKFAST' (only used if isSpecialEvent is true)
BoothShiftSlot.boothers: string[] — array of 3-4 names assigned to this shift

Candidate.notes: string — screening rationale, conversation notes, fit assessment
Candidate.reason: string — why flagged (research area, referral, etc.)

Budget.headcount: BudgetHeadcount — department headcount driving per-person cost calculations
Budget.summary: BudgetSummaryItem[] — rolled-up category totals (auto-calculated or manually entered)
Budget.teamSpend: TeamSpendItem[] — per-person or per-department spend breakdown with owner names

BudgetHeadcount.marketing: number — sometimes 0 if Marketing doesn't send attendees

BudgetLineItem.dateOfSpend: string — date when cost is incurred (e.g. '3/4/2026', '4/7/2026')
BudgetLineItem.source: string — payment method or vendor (e.g. 'Ramp Card', 'Freeman', 'Navan', 'Hotel Venue')
BudgetLineItem.costPerUnit: number — cost per person or per item
BudgetLineItem.quantity: number — number of units (people, nights, items, etc.)
BudgetLineItem.actualSpend: number — what was actually spent (filled in post-event)
BudgetLineItem.notes: string — critical context like 'Quantity is # of people x # of nights; Respective attendee's team is cost center' or '$359 for first license, $99 for second, $458 total' or '50/50 split between Recruiting/Marketing'

BudgetSummaryItem.category: string — e.g. 'Cost per person', 'Conference Sponsorship', 'Breakfast Event', 'Swag', 'Booth', 'Buffer Placeholder'
BudgetSummaryItem.notes: string — e.g. 'Paid for by respective cost center of each attendee', 'Research pays', '50/50 split between Recruiting/Marketing'

TeamSpendItem.owner: string — person's name who owns this budget line (e.g. 'Wei Zhan', 'Eitan Sheer')
TeamSpendItem.label: string — e.g. 'Total Research Spend', 'Total Eng Spend', 'Total Recruiting Spend', 'Total Marketing Spend'
TeamSpendItem.notes: string — e.g. 'Cost to send each attendee (flights, hotels, transportation, food, conference registration)', 'Breakfast event, 50/50 split with Marketing on conference booth and swag'

ActionItem.task: string — description of the action item
ActionItem.notes: string — detailed context, dependencies, instructions, handoff info
ActionItem.estimatedTime: string — e.g. 'Lauren = 6 hours', 'Recruiting: 2 hours', '30 minutes with Arturo'

default_action_items_template._description: Every new conference is initialized with these phases and action items. Users can add/remove/reorder.

Pre-Conference Setup.task: Work with Finance to get conference ramp card
Work with Finance to get onboarded onto expense platform
Share T&C legal updates with legal team

Content & Outreach Prep.task: Identify relevant papers and research to reference in outreach
Content & Outreach Prep.notes: Steps: verify keywords with research team, feed ~2500 papers into ChatGPT/Claude/Cursor to identify papers, then prompt engineering to double check and prevent errors

Content & Outreach Prep.task: Prioritize papers and authors for pre-conference outreach
Content & Outreach Prep.notes: Split between team members for review

Content & Outreach Prep.task: Create fake email accounts for outreach aliases
Content & Outreach Prep.notes: NOT aliases like last time — one for me to send bulk messages and an interest form for the breakfast. Could be worth sending out an interest form for the breakfast as well.
Two kinds: breakfast invite + booth awareness. No difference between P0 and P1 outreaches, too much administrative thrash.

Content & Outreach Prep.task: Finalize flyer copy and hand off to Design
Content & Outreach Prep.notes: Can reuse the one from CVPR with some modifications

Content & Outreach Prep.task: Complete booth build design and hand off to Design
Create a social media strategy with Marketing
Create a script to cross-reference authors with attendee list to find contact info

Logistics & Coordination.task: Get attendee list from conference organizer
Logistics & Coordination.notes: As a sponsor, we get a list of names and emails of attendees that we use for pre-conference outreach

Logistics & Coordination.task: Block off all calendars with relevant details, links, talking points
Logistics & Coordination.notes: Need to set up a training session. Might try to do most of it async and re-use the recordings.

Logistics & Coordination.task: Get service account from vendor for conference email
Finalize Gem/Google form for private event RSVP
Logistics & Coordination.notes: Waiting on vendor to answer a question about confirmation emails before sending out

Logistics & Coordination.task: Upload candidate list to Gem and categorize by project
Create and post job board requisitions on conference careers site
Logistics & Coordination.notes: Once published, put the link in the conference channel and tag DRI, going to print

Logistics & Coordination.task: Get scanning app set up for lead capture at booth
Collaborate with Design on private event invitation graphic
Logistics & Coordination.notes: Can reuse the one from CVPR with some modifications

Logistics & Coordination.task: Find contact info for top-priority authors using the attendee list
Logistics & Coordination.notes: Contingent on if we can access the resume book in advance of the conference. We will only need to find verifiable contact info for P0-P1 candidates that we want to invite to the breakfast event in advance (huge time savings). For everyone else, we can mass outreach from the resume book asking them to attend the booth.

Logistics & Coordination.task: Send mass outreach sequence to all attendees
Logistics & Coordination.notes: Now have figured out how to send. Waiting to send until we have the conference job posting live to link in the sequence. Also, CHANGE THE LINK IN THE BREAKFAST SEQUENCES FOR CAREERS PAGE LINK.

Logistics & Coordination.task: Manually remove people from breakfast interest sequence if they fill out the form
Logistics & Coordination.notes: If they reply back, it will stop automatically, but I'll need to stop the sequence manually if only the form is filled out, to my knowledge

Logistics & Coordination.task: Budget time to respond to email responses
Logistics & Coordination.notes: For P0 candidates, setting them up for on-prem chats with Wei, Yihan, Vickram + senior team

Event Week.task: Confirm freight delivery and booth materials arrived
Distribute booth staff schedule and shift reminders
Private event setup and vendor check-in
Run daily attendee debrief and lead capture sync
Capture photos and content for social / employer brand
Execute sourcing conversations on the floor
Monitor RSVP list and manage walk-ins at private event

Post-Conference.task: Send mass outreach sequence to all booth leads
Manually manage breakfast interest form responses
Upload all leads to Ashby with conference source tag
Send follow-up emails to all captured leads within 48 hours
Post-Conference.notes: Confirmation email for folks we definitely invite. Email letting some folks know we can't accommodate them. Email following up with folks who attended the breakfast afterwards to thank them + ask some specific folks to interview. Email following up with everyone we talked to at the booth to send careers page + resources (general).

Post-Conference.task: Debrief with team — what worked, what didn't
Post event recap on internal Slack (wins, metrics, photos)
Publish social content / blog post if applicable

default_budget_summary.notes: This includes: Hotel, flights, transportation, meals/incidentals, conference registration. Paid for by respective cost center of each attendee.

strategy.goals: Build Applied Intuition's talent brand in the AI research community, particularly in niche domains like computer vision. Convert high-quality research talent into active candidates. Increase Applied's visibility as a destination employer for researchers working on world foundation models, perception, and physical AI.
strategy.targetProfiles: Senior researchers, PhD candidates, and postdocs in computer vision, machine learning, robotics, and autonomous systems. Senior ICs at competing AV/AI companies. Faculty and lab leads who influence where their students go.
strategy.keyMessages: Physical AI — where everything that moves becomes intelligent. Applied's autonomy stack goes beyond chatbots into safety-critical systems. World Foundation Models are the next chapter after LLMs. You can't vibe code a 100-ton truck — the bar for physical AI is fundamentally higher.
strategy.successMetrics: Number of qualified leads sourced at conference. Breakfast event RSVP count and attendance rate. Post-event survey scores. Pipeline conversion rate at 30/60/90 days. Number of follow-up conversations booked on-site. Social content impressions from conference presence.

design.notes: Booth design must be submitted by May 11, 2026. Coordinate with Marketing/Design team. Should reflect the new Applied Intuition rebrand and Physical AI positioning.

privateEvent.notes:
Two breakfast events:
- Day 1: Saturday, June 6, 2026 — 8:30 AM to 11:00 AM
- Day 2: Sunday, June 7, 2026 — 8:30 AM to 11:00 AM

Format: Hosted breakfast with curated guest list. Goal is intimate setting for high-quality research talent conversations. Digital invites go out starting May 19.
